key_insight: A single dendritic compartment in a spiking neural network can perform in-context learning by implementing an online Widrow-Hoff LMS update directly in its subthreshold dynamics.
engineering_relevance: This suggests biologically plausible, single-layer SNNs may achieve general-purpose online adaptation without attention, deep architectures, or inference-time synaptic plasticity.
method: Use a single-layer compartmental spiking architecture (DendriCL).
Place the learning rule in apical dendritic recurrence rather than synaptic updates.
Exploit dynamics that are structurally equivalent to leaky online Widrow-Hoff LMS.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that DendriCL is seed-stable on super-dimensional Garg-2022 ICL tasks and that a linear probe recovers the reference online-LMS trajectory from the apical membrane with R² = 0.93.
watch_item: The result is benchmark-specific, so practical performance may depend on task dimension, training stability, and whether the dendritic dynamics can be implemented robustly in hardware or larger-scale SNNs.
paper_title: Dendritic In-Context Learning in a Single-Layer Spiking Neural Network
